audience_description: Lead teachers in Eduversal partner schools who hold both classroom-teaching responsibility AND department-level leadership: lesson plan oversight, walkthroughs of department teachers, departmental moderation, mentoring. Often serve as Cambridge subject lead for their discipline. Equivalent to Semesta's Head of Department role at the subject level.

regulatory_anchors.title: Permendikbud 6/2018
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Subject leader role under principal supervisi function.

regulatory_anchors.title: Permendikdasmen 11/2025 — Beban Kerja
regulatory_anchors.relevance: 37 jam 30 menit framework for the leader and the department teachers they oversee.

regulatory_anchors.title: Permendikdasmen 13/2025 — Pembelajaran Mendalam
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Deep learning approach across the subject scheme of work.

regulatory_anchors.title: PP 19/2005 jo. 13/2015 — 8 SNP
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Standar Isi, Standar Proses, Standar Penilaian — leader is accountable for these in their subject.

regulatory_anchors.title: Cambridge Handbook for Centres
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Cambridge subject lead responsibilities — syllabus delivery, internal moderation, candidate readiness.

framework_anchors.cambridge_teacher_standards_2023: Subject Leader is held to teacher standards plus the leadership-overlay tasks below.
framework_anchors.appraisal_framework_v2_1: Includes F3L (Leadership Responsibilities) — separate weighted framework for subject leaders.
framework_anchors.kpi_framework: teacher_kpi_config — leader holds dual evidence (own teaching + dept oversight)

recurring_tasks.title: Submit your own lesson plans for the coming week
recurring_tasks.description: Subject Leaders teach AND lead. Your own lessons must meet the same submission deadline you enforce on your team.
recurring_tasks.specific_timing: Friday by 16:00 of the preceding week
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: You set the tone. If you submit late, your department will too. Lead by example before you enforce.

recurring_tasks.title: Conduct 3-5 walkthroughs of department teachers this week
recurring_tasks.description: Minimum 3-5 walkthroughs (5-10 minutes each) across your department teachers. Every department teacher must be visited at least once every 14 days. Use the 'Look-Listen-Note' protocol and deliver 'I noticed / I wonder' feedback within 24 hours.
evidence_required: Walkthrough records in Central Academic Database
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: Walkthroughs are formative, NOT appraisal. Never mention domain levels in feedback notes. Use 'I noticed [specific scripted observation]' + 'I wonder [genuine reflective question]'. The 3:1 positive-to-corrective ratio is your discipline.
recurring_tasks.missed_threshold_action: Coverage gap (a teacher not visited in 14+ days) is flagged in your monthly report to the Academic Coordinator.

recurring_tasks.title: Enter all walkthrough data into the Central Academic Database within 24 hours
recurring_tasks.description: Walkthrough data, feedback note, and any flagged concerns logged in the database same-day if possible, max 24 hours after the visit.
recurring_tasks.specific_timing: End of day after each walkthrough
evidence_required: Database entries timestamped within 24h of visit
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: Late entries break the Domain Heatmap. The Academic Coordinator audits database freshness monthly. Don't accumulate entries — log immediately.

recurring_tasks.title: Review and approve department lesson plans for next week
recurring_tasks.description: Spot-check at least 20% of your department's submitted lesson plans (rolling). Use the LPQR — give Level 1 feedback within 5 working days. Focus alternates: WALT/WILT quality one week, Differentiation the next.
evidence_required: LPQR feedback in LMS for spot-checked plans
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: Don't read every plan every week — that's burnout. Rotate: 20% this week, different 20% next week. Every teacher gets a substantial review monthly. Use the LPQR as a shared standard, not a personal preference list.

recurring_tasks.title: Hold a 1:1 coaching conversation with at least one department teacher
recurring_tasks.description: Brief 15-30 minute coaching conversation with one teacher this week. Could be a follow-up to a walkthrough, IPDL goal review, or response to a Monday Morning Commitment that didn't land.
evidence_required: Brief conversation note in your IPDL
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: Coaching is not telling. Ask 'what did you notice?' before 'here's what I'd try'. Your teacher's growth is your appraisal evidence — invest the time.

recurring_tasks.title: Compliance & Administration weekly cycle
recurring_tasks.description: Same as for any teacher: attendance current, grades current, communications current. Plus department-level: walkthrough quota met, database current.

fortnightly_tasks.title: Verify every department teacher has been visited in the last 14 days
fortnightly_tasks.description: Run the Coverage Report from the Central Academic Database. Any teacher not visited in 14 days is a governance gap — visit before end of day.
evidence_required: Coverage Report attached to monthly LPQR submission

monthly_tasks.title: Facilitate the Week-4 Strategy PLC for your department
monthly_tasks.description: 90-minute PLC — you lead the agenda: Bright Spot (10) → Heatmap Trend Review (15) → Artifact Analysis (40) → Strategy Lab (15) → Monday Morning Commitment (10). Bring the anonymised Domain Heatmap and the selected student artifact 24 hours in advance.
evidence_required: PLC Impact Report submitted to Academic Coordinator first Monday of month
monthly_tasks.first_time_guide: 70% of meeting time MUST be instructional dialogue. If logistics drift past 30%, you've lost the plot. Read Section 7.10 (Common PLC Failures) before you facilitate your first PLC. Lead Learner — share your own Domain Heatmap data, including weak spots. Vulnerability from the top is the precondition for psychological safety.

monthly_tasks.title: Submit Lesson Plan Quality Report to the Academic Coordinator
monthly_tasks.description: First Monday of each month. LPQR trends across the department, teachers requiring a Planning Support Plan, exemplar plans shared in the PLC.

monthly_tasks.title: Submit monthly PLC Impact Report
monthly_tasks.description: Was last month's Monday Morning Commitment implemented? Verified in walkthroughs? What's next month's focus? File to Principal/Coordinator first Monday.

monthly_tasks.title: Joint Walkthrough with Principal or Academic Coordinator
monthly_tasks.description: Once per month, conduct a Joint Walkthrough — Principal/Coord and you observe the same lesson independently, then compare notes. Discrepancies resolved against the rubric. Calibration is the discipline that prevents subjectivity drift.
monthly_tasks.first_time_guide: If your scoring diverges from the Principal's by more than one level on any domain, that's the conversation. Don't defend your score — investigate the divergence.

monthly_tasks.title: Monthly student work review across the department
monthly_tasks.description: Sample student work across teachers in your department. Identify outcome trends — what concepts are students mastering, what are they struggling with?
evidence_required: Trend findings filed in PLC Artifact Log

termly_tasks.title: Lead internal moderation and standardisation session
termly_tasks.description: Mid-Sem and end-of-Sem. Cross-marking session with department teachers. Blind-marking calibration. Resolve marking discrepancies — divergence >2 grade boundaries on the same script requires a third marker.

termly_tasks.title: Termly Cambridge syllabus review (if Cambridge subject)
termly_tasks.description: Within 4 weeks of Cambridge results release: review examiner reports for the previous series, identify required adjustments to schemes of work, communicate changes to the department.
termly_tasks.applies_when: subject is Cambridge IGCSE / AS-A / Checkpoint

termly_tasks.title: Termly IPDL review with each department teacher
termly_tasks.description: Twice per year, sit with each teacher in your department for a structured IPDL review. Are goals being met? What support is needed? Adjust goals where evidence demands it.
evidence_required: IPDL signed off by you and the teacher

1.focus: Department alignment, scheme of work confirmation, expectations reset

tasks.title: Hold department year-start briefing
tasks.description: All department teachers meet for 60-90 minutes. Cover: scheme of work for the year, weekly cadence expectations, PLC schedule, collaborative-planning protocol, the LPQR, walkthrough rhythm, mentor pairings.
tasks.first_time_guide: This is your tone-setter. Be explicit about every rhythm — walkthroughs are formative, PLC is evidence-based, lesson plans are due Friday 16:00. Don't assume any of it; state all of it.

tasks.title: Produce Dual-Framework Curriculum Map for the subject
tasks.description: Map your subject's scheme of work against BOTH the Cambridge syllabus AND the national curriculum (Kurikulum Merdeka). Identify alignment points and divergences. Submit to Academic Coordinator within 2 weeks.
tasks.first_time_guide: Where the two frameworks align (most of the year), one well-designed lesson serves both. Where they diverge, escalate to the Coordinator — don't resolve independently.

2.focus: Mentor pairings, first walkthroughs, IPDL goal-setting

tasks.title: Pair every new teacher with a Level 4 mentor
tasks.description: If your department has new teachers, pair each with a Level 4 (Exemplary) returning teacher within the same or adjacent subject area. Ground the relationship in the Mentor-Mentee Contract.
evidence_required: Mentor pairings filed with Academic Coordinator

3.focus: Establish walkthrough rhythm, baseline domain impressions

tasks.title: Complete first walkthrough of every department teacher
tasks.description: By end of W3, every department teacher should have received at least one walkthrough — establishing your baseline impression and signalling that the rhythm is real.

8.focus: Sign off all Cambridge candidate entries for the subject

tasks.title: Final sign-off of Oct/Nov candidate list (if Cambridge subject)
tasks.description: Cross-check with Cambridge Coordinator: every student entered for the right paper, option, tier. Sign off in writing.

tasks.title: Lead EASE I administration for your subject
tasks.description: Coordinate timing, conditions, and submission of EASE I papers across all department teachers. Verify protocol compliance.

tasks.title: Lead end-of-Sem-1 moderation session for the department
tasks.description: Cross-marking, blind-marking, and discrepancy resolution. Critical for grading integrity. Outcome feeds into Section 9.10 Grading Integrity Audit.

tasks.title: Spot-check report card comments across the department
tasks.description: Read 20% of your department's report comments before they go to parents. Reject 'good progress' generic comments — return for revision with named Next Step.

tasks.title: Conduct mid-year IPDL review with every department teacher
tasks.description: Are goals on track? What evidence do they have? What's needed for Sem-2? Adjust goals where evidence demands it.
evidence_required: IPDL signed mid-year by you and each teacher

tasks.title: Analyse Cambridge Oct/Nov results for your subject
tasks.description: Compare your students' grades against grade boundaries, examiner reports, and school predictions. Identify question-level patterns. Update scheme of work where examiner report indicates a curricular gap.

tasks.title: Sign off May/June Cambridge candidate list
tasks.description: Cross-check with Cambridge Coordinator: every student entered correctly.

tasks.title: Lead EASE II administration for your subject
tasks.description: Coordinate timing, conditions, and submission of EASE II papers across all department teachers.

tasks.title: Update Vertical Curriculum Map for your subject
tasks.description: Identify prerequisite gaps, redundancies, cognitive plateaus across year groups in your subject. Submit updated map to Academic Coordinator for Annual Vertical Gap Analysis.
evidence_required: Vertical map filed in Governance Archive

tasks.title: Lead end-of-Sem-2 moderation
tasks.description: Final summative moderation. Records feed into the Annual Grading Integrity Audit conducted by the Principal.

tasks.title: Prepare appraisal evidence packs for every department teacher
tasks.description: For each teacher: their walkthrough trend, LPQR average, PLC participation, Monday Commitment implementation rate, IPDL summary. Hand over to whoever conducts their formal appraisal (could be you, could be Academic Coordinator).

tasks.title: Conduct annual appraisal for each department teacher you supervise
tasks.description: Full 5-step Clinical Supervision Cycle for any formal appraisals you're scheduled to lead. IIPs signed by you and the teacher.

tasks.title: Archive department records and draft next-year plan
tasks.description: Department PLC minutes, walkthrough records, moderation samples archived. Draft Sem-1 PD priorities for next year based on this year's Domain Heatmap trends.

extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.title: Monthly mentor-leader meeting with Academic Coordinator
extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.description: First-year subject leaders meet with the Academic Coordinator monthly. Bring leadership questions, coaching dilemmas, and PLC planning concerns.

extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.title: Subject Leader certification — Mentor Base + Coaching Module
extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.description: Complete the Eduversal Mentor Base certification + Subject Leader Coaching module within first 12 weeks. Requires for ongoing oversight role.

feeds_into: Academic Coordinator's Institutional Risk Assessment Report

provenance.framework_sources: Cambridge Teacher Standards 2023 + Leadership tier, Eduversal Teacher Appraisal Framework v2.1 (F3L for leaders), Eduversal Teacher Competency Framework
provenance.operational_pattern_source: Adapted from Semesta School Academic Leadership Governance Handbook 2026-2027 — specifically the HoD role definition (Sections 2.4, 4.8, 6.6, 7.4) — with Eduversal-network adjustments
